title: Traffic and undercut neighbor features
summary: F1 pit timing depends on the cars immediately ahead/behind, not just a driver's tyre age.
rationale: F1 pit timing depends on the cars immediately ahead/behind, not just a driver's tyre age. Undercut/overcut decisions are triggered by relative pace, track position, and tyre offset to nearby rivals; current best only uses median lap pace and tyre-life rank, missing local competitive context.
implementation_hint: Within each Race-LapNumber, sort by Position and add features for ahead/behind driver TyreLife, Compound, LapTime_Delta, Pace_Deviation, Position gap proxy, and whether nearby rivals pitted recently via PitStop/Stint change. Also add field counts of cars on older/newer tyres by compound.
expected_effect: Likely improves AUC on borderline pit calls where strategy is driven by traffic rather than absolute degradation.
risk: Feature computation must preserve original row order and avoid using future laps; if Position is noisy after pit cycles, neighbor signals may add variance.
